data.title: Texas Instruments Stock (TXN) Soars on Analyst Price Target Increases
data.description:
Texas Instruments (TXN) stock saw major gains on Thursday after the semiconductor company received several analyst updates following its latest earnings report.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Let's dig into the latest updates from Wall Street's top analysts today.

  • Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider reiterated a Sell rating with a price target of $200, representing a possible 28.55% downside.
  • Wells Fargo analyst Joseph Quatrochi reiterated a Hold rating with a price target of $260, representing a possible 7.12% downside.
  • TD Cowen analyst Joshua Buchalter was assigned a Buy rating with a price target of $300, representing a possible 7.17% upside.
  • Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore reiterated a Sell rating with a price target of $221, representing a possible 21.05% downside.
  • Robert W. Baird analyst Tristan Gerra reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $300, representing a possible 7.17% upside.
  • Wolfe Research analyst Chris Caso reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $315, representing a possible 12.53% upside.
  • Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya upgraded the stock to a Buy rating with a price target of $320, representing a possible 14.31% upside.
  • KeyBanc analyst John Vinh reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $325, representing a possible 16.10% upside.
  • Rosenblatt Securities analyst Kevin Cassidy was assigned a Buy rating with a price target of $330, representing a possible 17.89% upside.
  • Benchmark Company analyst Cody Acree reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $315, representing a possible 12.53% upside.
  • Stifel Nicolaus analyst Tore Svanberg reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $290, representing a possible 3.60% upside.
  • Barclays analyst Thomas O'Malley upgraded the stock to a Hold rating with a price target of $250, representing a possible 10.69% downside.
  • Susquehanna analyst Christopher Rolland reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $300, representing a possible 7.17% upside.
  • J.P. Morgan analyst Harlan Sur reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $280, representing a possible 0.03% upside.
  • Truist Financial analyst William Stein was assigned a Hold rating with a price target of $278, representing a possible 0.69% downside.
  • Mizuho Securities analyst Vijay Rakesh reiterated a Hold rating with a price target of $255, representing a possible 8.91% downside.
  • Citi analyst Atif Malik was assigned a Buy rating with a price target of $280, representing a possible 0.03% upside.
  • UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri reiterated a Buy rating with a price target of $295, representing a possible 5.38% upside.
  • Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Matthew Prisco was assigned a Hold rating with a price target of $280, representing a possible 0.03% upside.
  • Jefferies analyst Blayne Curtis reiterated a Hold rating with a price target of $260, representing a possible 7.12% downside.

Texas Instruments Stock Movement Today

Texas Instruments stock was up 19.34% on Thursday, extending a 63.62% year-to-date rally. The stock has also increased 45.85% over the past 12 months.

TXN stock also experienced heavy trading activity today, as some 13 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 5.74 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: S&P 500 Slips as Iran Holds Off on Peace Talks
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading slightly lower on Thursday as Iran has continued to signal that it will not engage in peace talks until the U.S. lifts its blockade on vessels entering and exiting Iranian ports. Earlier this week, President Trump extended the ceasefire between the two sides in order to give Iran's "fractured" leadership time to submit a proposal.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

"The infighting is between the "Hardliners," who have been losing BADLY on the battlefield, and the "Moderates," who are not very moderate at all (but gaining respect!), is CRAZY!" Trump said in a Truth Social post.

Hormuz Disruptions Fuel Oil and Inflation Gains

Tensions at Hormuz remain elevated. Since the beginning of the blockade, the U.S. has directed 31 vessels to turn around or return to their ports. Meanwhile, both sides have continued to seize ships in or around the waterway.

Brent crude futures (BZ), the global benchmark for oil, are up by 1% on the day and 33% since the war began on February 28. The dramatic rise has also raised the risk of higher inflation, with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) surging 0.9% in March, its largest monthly gain since June 2022.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump Administration Rethinks Ban on Anthropic after Mythos AI Debut
data.description:
The Trump administration is signaling a shift in its stance toward Anthropic following the preview of the company's new Mythos AI model. The change comes after months of tension following the Pentagon's ban on Anthropic and the company's designation as a national supply chain risk. In response to the label, Anthropic has filed two lawsuits against the Pentagon and is challenging the decision through legal and regulatory channels, arguing that the designation is unjustified.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for NVDA bears

Trump Says Anthropic Is "Shaping Up," Signaling Future Deals

In a recent CNBC interview, U.S. President Donald Trump suggested that Anthropic's AI models could still be used in government operations. He said that the AI company was "shaping up," and indicated that future deals could be in the works. 

Trump also noted that discussions had recently taken place at the White House, with talks with the Pentagon still underway. This comes after an earlier move by the administration directing federal agencies to stop using Anthropic's technology, citing national security concerns and effectively classifying the company as a potential risk at the time.

However, despite that restriction, some government bodies, including the U.S. Department of the Treasury (TREAS) and the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), had already been quietly testing Anthropic's Mythos model.

Around the same time, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei met with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles on April 17 to discuss Mythos AI. Both parties had described the session as "productive," with the meeting focused on cyber defense and potential collaborations that could provide the government access to the AI model.

Mythos Cyber Capabilities Prompt Anthropic Ban Reconsideration

The review into Anthropic's national supply risk status is believed to be closely tied to the capabilities of the Mythos AI model. A preview of the model was recently launched, showing its ability to detect software vulnerabilities and help organizations fix critical system flaws before attackers can exploit them. 

Because of these advantages, Anthropic opted for a controlled rollout under a new initiative called Project Glasswing. The company invited a select group of partners, including banks like JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Goldman Sachs (GS), as well as cybersecurity firms, to stress-test the system and develop safeguards.

By restricting access and emphasizing defensive use cases, Anthropic has sought to demonstrate that AI can be deployed responsibly. For policymakers, the model's capabilities also underscore how advanced AI tools are becoming increasingly important to cybersecurity, an area where the Pentagon faces constant and evolving threats.

Which AI Stocks are a Strong Buy?

While Anthropic remains a private company with no publicly traded stock yet, investors interested in AI stocks can explore well-known players such as Nvidia (NVDA), Microsoft (MSFT), and Meta Platforms (META). All of these stocks have been rated Strong Buys by various Wall Street analysts. To get more information on their performance ratings and price targets, visit TipRanks' Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Microsoft Offers Voluntary Buyouts to Employees for the First Time
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) is offering voluntary buyouts to a small group of employees for the first time in its history. According to CNBC, the program is aimed at U.S. workers at the senior director level and below whose age and years of service add up to at least 70. Eligible employees and their managers will receive more details on May 7, although those on sales incentive plans will not be able to participate. According to the company, the goal is to give long-tenured employees the option to retire with support on their own terms.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Interestingly, this decision comes as Microsoft continues to spend heavily on artificial intelligence. The company is investing more in data centers to support cloud customers that are using AI, similar to what Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) are doing. In addition, Microsoft has already reduced costs through several rounds of layoffs, and it had about 228,000 employees as of June 2025.

On top of that, Microsoft is changing how it rewards employees. The company will no longer require managers to directly tie stock awards to cash bonuses, thereby giving them more flexibility to reward strong performance. It is also simplifying the review process by reducing the number of pay options managers can choose from. As a result, these changes are meant to make compensation easier to manage while supporting the company's shift toward AI.

What Is the Price Target for Microsoft?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 34 Buys and two Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average MSFT price target of $573.99 per share implies 37% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: IBM Stock Plunges as Earnings Report Fails to Ease AI Fears
data.description:
Shares of tech giant IBM (IBM) are plunging today as they join the broader sell-off across the software industry. This sharp decline came after the company reported its first-quarter results, which did little to ease investor concerns about AI. While IBM's software segment performed better than expected, its consulting business fell short of estimates, which weighed on overall sentiment. As a result, the mixed performance raised fresh questions about the company's growth outlook.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Trade IBM with leverage

At the same time, IBM's guidance failed to impress investors. The company maintained its full-year forecast of more than 5% revenue growth in constant currency, versus analysts' estimates of 5.4%. Because of this, some investors viewed the outlook as cautious, especially after IBM recently completed its acquisition of Confluent, which is expected to add to revenue. In addition, concerns about how artificial intelligence could disrupt traditional software business models have been putting pressure on the entire sector.

Even so, IBM has pushed back against these concerns. The company argues that AI will actually support its business rather than hurt it. CEO Arvind Krishna said that customers' increasing AI use is becoming a positive driver for IBM's global operations. In addition, IBM has been improving its business through acquisitions, which include Red Hat, HashiCorp, and, most recently, Confluent, as it shifts toward hybrid cloud software. However, investor worries about AI disruption and slower growth continue to weigh on the stock.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $305.06 per share implies 31.5% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Akanda Corp Stock Blasts Off as Cannabis King Secures a 200km Fiber Expansion in Mexico
data.description:
Investors are witnessing a historic surge in Akanda Corp (AKAN) stock on Thursday. The company's share price has exploded, jumping over 214% in a single day. This massive leap comes as the business pivots from a pure focus on cannabis and hemp toward becoming a powerhouse in telecommunications infrastructure. Traders are flocking to the stock as the firm reinvents itself to capture new profits in the Mexican data market.

data.title: Applied Digital Stock (APLD) Surges 14% on Securing $7.5B AI Data Center Lease with U.S. Hyperscaler
data.description:
Applied Digital (APLD) surged more than 14% on Thursday after the company announced a $7.5 billion, 15‑year lease agreement with a new U.S.-based, high investment‑grade hyperscaler. This marks one of the largest deals in its history and reflects its AI‑focused data center strategy.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for APLD bulls/bears

The deal covers 300 megawatts (MW) of critical IT load at Applied Digital's Delta Forge 1 AI Factory, a 430‑MW campus built for large‑scale AI and high-performance computing. The new tenant becomes the company's second U.S. investment-grade hyperscaler, boosting Applied Digital long‑term revenue visibility.

With this deal, Applied Digital's contracted lease revenue now tops $23 billion, and more than half of it is backed by investment-grade customers.

"We remain focused on delivering operational AI capacity at scale," said CEO Wes Cummins. He called the agreement an important step in diversifying the company's customer base and improving revenue stability.

Additional Financing in Motion

Alongside the lease announcement, Applied Digital said it expects to secure up to $300 million in bridge financing to continue developing the 150-MW Building 3 at its Polaris Forge 1 campus, as well as up to $300 million in revolving credit to support development across its platform.

Both financing facilities are expected to close soon and will be provided by a syndicate of bank lenders.

Major Win in the AI Infrastructure Race

The deal arrives as demand for AI-optimized data centers accelerates across major cloud and tech companies. By securing another blue-chip hyperscaler, Applied Digital positions itself as a key infrastructure provider in the rapidly expanding AI compute market.

Investors responded quickly, sending the stock sharply higher as the agreement points to both long‑term, meaningful growth potential.

Is APLD a Strong Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on APLD stock based on seven Buys assigned in the last three months. Further, the average Applied Digital price target of $46.67 per share implies 26.78% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Microsoft Will Report Q3 Earnings on April 29 — Here's Who Owns MSFT Stock
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) is set to report its fiscal third-quarter results on April 29. So far this year, the stock is down about 14%, pressured by heavy AI infrastructure spending, rising competition, and broader macro uncertainty. For Q3, analysts expect earnings per share (EPS) of $4.06 on revenue of $81.3 billion, up from $3.46 per share on $70.1 billion in the same period last year. In this article, we take a closer look at Microsoft's ownership structure and what it may signal for investors.

data.title: White House Accuses China of 'Industrial-Scale' AI Theft
data.description:
The White House's Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) has accused foreign entities operating in China of running distillation campaigns in order to steal American AI technology. Distillation refers to the process where a smaller, less complex AI model is trained to copy the behavior and knowledge of a larger, more advanced model.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

"These foreign entities are using tens of thousands of proxies and jailbreaking techniques in coordinated campaigns to systematically extract American breakthroughs," said OSTP Director Michael Kratsios on Thursday.

AI Replication Concerns Emerge ahead of U.S.–China Talks

The release of China's DeepSeek last year sparked concerns that it may have benefited from the capabilities of leading AI models in the U.S., intensifying fears surrounding model replication and intellectual property risks.

The accusation comes ahead of President Trump's visit to Beijing, China, on May 14 to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping and could lead to renewed tensions between the two superpowers.
data.author: Eddie Pan
